subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 04
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering space, human body, community, and global facts for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets combined.
questions.hint: It is the fifth planet from the Sun.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: An adult human has 206 bones in their skeleton.
questions.hint: It is a number between 200 and 210.
questions.objective: Learn basic facts about the human body.

questions.q: Which of these is a primary color?
questions.explanation: The three primary colors are red, yellow, and blue.
questions.hint: It is the color of a ripe strawberry.

questions.q: What do bees collect from flowers to make honey?
questions.explanation: Bees collect nectar from flowers, which they then process into honey.
questions.hint: It is a sweet liquid produced by plants.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their activities.

questions.explanation: Antarctica is the coldest, windiest, and driest continent on Earth.
questions.hint: It is located at the very bottom of the globe.

questions.explanation: Thomas Edison is famously credited with developing the first commercially practical incandescent light bulb.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy snack?
questions.explanation: Fresh fruit contains natural vitamins, fiber, and nutrients, making it a healthy choice.
questions.hint: It grows on trees or plants and is sweet.

questions.q: What do we call a person who travels into space?
questions.explanation: An astronaut is a person trained to command, pilot, or serve as a crew member of a spacecraft.

questions.q: What is the capital of the United States?
questions.explanation: Washington D.C. is the capital city of the United States.
questions.hint: It is named after the first US president.

questions.q: Which type of animal is a shark?
questions.explanation: Sharks are a group of elasmobranch fish characterized by a cartilaginous skeleton.
questions.hint: They have gills to breathe underwater.
